title: urginea insularis
intro: Urginea insularis, also known as the Madeiran squill or Island squill, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asparagaceae, native to the island of Madeira in the Atlantic Ocean. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antispasmodic and expectorant effects, which have been used to treat conditions such as asthma and bronchitis. The health benefits of Urginea insularis include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory function, making it a potentially valuable remedy for conditions such as chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD). Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made from its rhizomes, which are dried and processed into extracts or tinctures that can be used to treat a range of health issues.
